summary: Matched a broad set of Dolphin SDK/library code by moving more functionality out of raw asm into C, especially OS cache/memory/reboot code, DSP task assertion logic, and portions of PAD/PADClamp. The PR also added/expanded headers and named several data symbols so remaining asm became more searchable and interoperable with C. Evidence for the 'from prime' source is limited to the PR title; the diff itself shows concrete Dolphin SDK matching and symbol cleanup.
what_changed: Deleted asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s and implemented DSPAssertTask in src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c, with typed DSPTaskInfo externs for __DSP_curr_task, __DSP_first_task, __DSP_last_task, __DSP_tmp_task, __DSP_rude_task, and __DSP_rude_task_pending.
Reworked DSP register access to use __DSPRegs and OS_INTR_DSP_DSP instead of raw HW_REG constants and numeric interrupt 7.
Substantially rewrote src/dolphin/os/OSCache.c, replacing many commented asm blocks with concise inline asm functions and C helpers/constants for cache/L2/DMA error handling.
Removed asm-only data files OSCache_data.s, OSMemory_data.s, and OSReboot_data.s by moving data into C declarations or statics.
Converted OSMemory reset/protection handling toward C: added __MEMRegs, static OnReset, OSResetFunctionInfo lbl_80402348, and C MEMIntrruptHandler using OS_ERROR_PROTECTION.
Renamed OSReboot data from anonymous labels to C statics Header, SaveStart, SaveEnd, and Prepared, and updated asm references accordingly.
Added include/dolphin/os/OSSerial.h with SI channel, error, type, and wireless constants plus prototypes for SIProbe, SIGetTypeString, SIRefreshSamplingRate, and SISetSamplingRate.

Added src/dolphin/pad/Pad.c implementing PADEnable, PADDisable, DoReset, UpdateOrigin, PADOriginCallback, PADOriginUpdateCallback, and PADProbeCallback while leaving PADTypeAndStatusCallback and other code in asm.
Added src/dolphin/pad/PadClamp.c with PADClampRegion data and ClampStick/PADClamp logic, shrinking asm/dolphin/pad/Padclamp.s.

Renamed several remaining asm data/function symbols: ExiHandler, DbgHandler, TxHandler, RxHandler, ExiCallback, __PADFixBits, Type, Origin, ResettingChan, AnalogMode, Spec, Initialized, EnabledBits, ResettingBits, RecalibrateBits, WaitingBits, CheckingBits, PendingBits, SamplingRate, SamplingCallback, and recalibrated.
Updated include/dolphin/pad/pad.h with dolphin/types.h, PAD_CHANMAX, PAD_ERR_NONE, PAD_SPEC_2, and a typed PADClamp(PADStatus*) prototype.
Updated obj_files.mk to add src/dolphin/pad/Pad.c.o and PadClamp.c.o and remove now-obsolete asm/data objects.
Added a Makefile Frank rule for src/dolphin/pad C objects, matching the existing dolphin/card object-fixing pattern.
smart_moves: Moved only matched or matchable subsets of PAD into C while retaining still-needed asm for the rest of Pad.s, reducing risk in a large module rather than forcing an all-at-once rewrite.
Introduced OSSerial.h before using SI constants in PAD code, replacing magic error/type bits with reusable names that future SI/PAD work can share.
Turned anonymous data labels into meaningful C statics where possible, allowing deletion of separate *_data.s files and making ownership/locality clearer.
Kept low-level cache/BAT functions as inline asm but cleaned them into named parameters, local labels, and SDK-style constants, preserving exact instruction control while improving readability.
Used typed externs such as DSPTaskInfo* instead of void* to make field accesses like task->priority and task->state explicit and safer.
Extended the Frank build fixup path to dolphin/pad C objects, acknowledging that matched PAD C needed the same profile/vanilla object reconciliation mechanism already used elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling SDK libraries, create shared headers for hardware constants early; it lets newly matched C code avoid duplicated magic numbers and makes later files easier to match.
Anonymous data asm can often be retired by introducing C statics with the same placement-sensitive names and then updating remaining asm references to those names.
Partial asm-to-C conversion is valuable: implementing callbacks and helpers in C while leaving hard functions in asm still shrinks the unmatched surface and improves symbol context.
For memory-mapped hardware, volatile fixed-address arrays such as __MEMRegs or existing register arrays can make C match while documenting register banks better than raw literal addresses.

Low-level cache primitives may remain inline asm, but the decomp win comes from replacing address-comment dumps with concise assembly using semantic constants like HID0_DCE, L2CR_L2E, and HID2_* error bits.
Reset callbacks and OS error handlers should use SDK-style typed signatures and named error IDs; OSMemory's switch to OnReset(BOOL) and OS_ERROR_PROTECTION is a useful pattern.
Build-system cleanup must accompany object migration: remove deleted asm/data objects and add new C objects in obj_files.mk, then add any object post-processing rules needed by the module.
naming_conventions: Dolphin SI constants use SI_* prefixes: SI_MAX_CHAN, SI_ERROR_NO_RESPONSE, SI_CHAN_BIT(chan), SI_TYPE_GC, SI_GC_WAVEBIRD, and SI_WIRELESS_*.
PAD constants use PAD_* prefixes: PAD_CHANMAX, PAD_ERR_NONE, PAD_SPEC_2, PAD_CHAN0_BIT.
Private SDK/global implementation symbols often keep double-underscore names, as with __PADFixBits, __DSP_curr_task, __DSP_rude_task_pending, __MEMRegs, and __OSErrorTable.
Former anonymous HIO labels were renamed to role-based handler names: ExiHandler, DbgHandler, TxHandler, RxHandler, and ExiCallback.

Former PAD data labels were renamed to concise state names: Type, Origin, ResettingChan, XPatchBits, AnalogMode, Spec, EnabledBits, ResettingBits, WaitingBits, CheckingBits, PendingBits, SamplingRate, SamplingCallback, and recalibrated.
OSReboot bss/sbss labels became file-local semantic statics: Header, SaveStart, SaveEnd, Prepared.
Hardware/cache bit definitions were named after registers and fields, e.g. MSR_IR, MSR_DR, L2CR_L2E, L2CR_L2I, SRR1_DMA_BIT, HID0_ICE, HID0_DCE, HID2_DCHERR, HID2_DNCERR, HID2_DCMERR, HID2_DQOERR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced large commented asm blocks in OSCache.c with compact inline asm using register parameters such as register void* addr and register size_t nbytes.
Used local inline-asm labels like @1 and @2 instead of global lbl_* labels where functions stayed as asm.
Preserved exact low-level instructions for cache operations, including dcbi, dcbf, dcbst, dcbz, icbi, sync/sc, mtctr, bdnz, and SPR access.
Kept BAT/MMU configuration functions in OSMemory.c as asm but cleaned formatting and removed pragma/comment noise around the same instruction sequences.

Matched DSPAssertTask in C by structuring the control flow around interrupt disable/restore, current-task equality, priority comparison, rude-task pending state, and a helper for the hardware command.
Matched PAD origin adjustment with a switch on AnalogMode & 0x700 and explicit field masking/subtraction on PADStatus fields.
Reduced asm file sizes by deleting matched functions from Pad.s and Padclamp.s while keeping remaining unmatched functions and data in asm with improved symbol names.
Adjusted object lists to avoid duplicate definitions once C statics replaced separate *_data.s objects.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.actionable_lesson: No substantive reviewer feedback was present in the provided slice; only a rebase note is evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish decompiling asm/dolphin/pad/Pad.s after PADTypeAndStatusCallback.
Find later cleanup of OSSerial.h constants and SI prototypes.
Find PRs that rename remaining PAD globals such as Type, Origin, EnabledBits, and PendingBits into headers.
Find later OSCache matching changes involving LCEnable, L2Enable, DMAErrorHandler, or __OSCacheInit.
Find review or commit context explaining the 'from prime' source mentioned in the PR title.
